content.problemStatement:
You and KKK of your friends gather for dinner at a restaurant, and the total bill is Rs. NNN. You pay the entire bill, and since everyone shared the food equally, the bill needs to be split evenly among you all.

After asking your friends to reimburse their fair shares, each person pays you back. However, if the amount to be paid back wasn't an integer, they round down to the previous integer value.

For example, if each friend should have paid Rs. 13.213.213.2, they instead pay Rs. 131313. Even if they should have paid Rs. 13.7913.7913.79, they still only pay Rs. 131313, rounding down.

Calculate the net amount that you ended up paying after everyone settles their debts.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of one line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the net amount you paid.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers N and K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the net amount you end up paying after all friends have settled.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: People = 2, each share = floor(500/2)=250, friend pays 250, net = 500-250=250.\nTest case 2: People = 10, each share = floor(999/10)=99, friends pay 9*99=891, net = 999-891=108.\nTest case 3: People = 5, each share = floor(1/5)=0, friends pay 0, net = 1.
Test case 1: People = 4, share = floor(120/4)=30, friends pay 3*30=90, net = 120-90=30.\nTest case 2: People = 10, share = floor(250/10)=25, friends pay 9*25=225, net = 250-225=25.

solution.approach: For each test case, there are K+1 people (you and K friends). The fair share per person is N/(K+1). Each friend pays the integer part of this value, i.e., floor(N/(K+1)). The total amount you receive from friends is K * floor(N/(K+1)). Your net payment is therefore N - K * floor(N/(K+1)). The computation uses only integer division, runs in O(1) per test case, and requires O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        K = int(next(it))
        per = N // (K + 1)
        net = N - K * per
        out_lines.append(str(net))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
